violations.text: Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Chicken was placed hot in the prep unit in covered container greater than four inches deep. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/01/2025
Coving in the area near the ice machine is in poor repair, with a section missing . The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 01/12/2026

The floor in the ice machine area has an accumulation of soil residue and debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 12/01/2025
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 12/04/2024
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/04/2024

Food contact surfaces of hanging utensils were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/04/2024

Ham deli slices were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Product was disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/04/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Chicken soup was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep (plastic covered plastic 22 quart Cambro). Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/04/2024

Observed insect control device above milk cooler where ice cream scoops were stored for service. Dead insects observed on top of milk cooler. Device should be removed, or no food prep in area. New Violation. Correct By: 12/04/2024
Utensils (ice cream scoops) were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. New Violation. Correct By: 12/04/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the table beneath the flattop grill have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. The ice cream chest freezer has an accumulation of frost and debris. New Violation. Correct By: 12/04/2024
The corner wall near the dry storage shelf is in poor repair, with the edge trim piece broken off. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 12/14/2024
Ice cream in the chest freezer was adulterated with frozen condensate & debris. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/26/2024

Food contact surfaces of the ice machine were observed with an accumulation of biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized, and ice disposed of. New Violation. Correct By: 06/26/2024

Pasta, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/26/2024

Pasta was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/26/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Pasta was placed hot in the refrigerator in covered plastic container greater than four inches deep. Discussed placing a rolling rack (or half rack) inside the walk in, designated as the cooling rack. Foods are to be cooled inside the walk in cooler or freezer. New Violation. Correct By: 06/26/2024

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 06/26/2024

The door to the outside, located in the loading dock, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 07/06/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: can opener base shelf above food prep table cutting board table oven table below flattop grill ice machine The ice cream chest freezer has an accumulation of frost & debris. New Violation. Correct By: 06/28/2024

The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 08/29/2023
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. English Responding to Vomiting and Diarrhea Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/29/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener and mixer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of the ice machines (kitchen, dining room, kitchenette) were observed with an accumulation of soil residue. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Items were taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 08/29/2023

Chicken stock, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/29/2023

Pasta was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Turkey was marked with 2 date mark stickers, 4 days apart. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Pasta was disposed of. New Violation. Correct By: 08/29/2023

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Chicken broth was placed hot in the walk in cooler in a covered plastic container greater than four inches deep. Pasta was placed hot in the refrigerator in a covered plastic container greater than 4 inches deep. Cooling logs are required to be maintained for all cooled foods for inspector to review at next inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 08/29/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: can opener base mixer steamer table mixer table 8 burner range table beneath flattop grill plate warmer unit table beneath window New Violation. Correct By: 08/29/2023
One light bulb beneath the hood is burned out and needs to be replaced. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/03/2023

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 08/29/2023
